text.title: ES Fundamentals Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules establishing the invariants of an Event-Sourced system.

rule.statement: Events MUST be immutable once stored. The event log MUST be append-only: no UPDATE, no DELETE on event rows. Correction events (`OrderPriceCorrected`) replace destructive edits.
rule.rationale: Mutable events corrupt every downstream projection + snapshot derived from them; rebuilds become non-deterministic.

rule.statement: Current state MUST NOT be stored directly. State MUST be derived by replaying the event sequence. Snapshots are caches per `[[event-sourcing-snapshots]]` — they MUST always be rebuildable from offset 0.
rule.rationale: Storing current state alongside events creates two sources of truth that drift; bugs become unfixable when they disagree.

rule.statement: Events MUST be applied in stream order (by version number). Concurrent commands MUST go through `expected_version`-guarded append. Reordered replay is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Out-of-order replay produces invalid state; expected_version guards optimistic writes against concurrent commits.

rule.statement: Event class names MUST be past-tense business facts (`OrderPlaced`, `PaymentCaptured`, `ItemAdded`). Names like `UpdateOrder`, `SetStatus`, `OrderStateChanged` are forbidden — they erase business intent.
rule.rationale: Past-tense names communicate "what happened" and survive refactors; procedural names rot when state shapes evolve.

rule.statement: Each aggregate instance MUST have its own event stream keyed by aggregate identity. Cross-aggregate streams (e.g., one stream for all orders) are forbidden — they break the consistency boundary.
rule.rationale: Shared streams force lock contention and prevent independent replay; aggregate-scoped streams keep load O(events per aggregate).
